data.content:
Plan review: approved.

The plan directly targets the reproduced failure mode: browser HTTP/1.1 connection exhaustion from one EventSource per running terminal session, plus hidden-session parse/buffer pressure. A multiplexed SSE endpoint preserves background terminal events and approval/failure behavior better than an active-session-only fallback. The buffer bounds and diagnostic script cover the memory/debuggability requirements. The proposed tests cover the new routing/buffering behavior, and the manual/CDP probe covers the browser-level connection-budget behavior that unit tests cannot faithfully reproduce.

No knot invariants are violated: the work stays in terminal/browser streaming code, does not alter Knots workflow semantics, does not lower maxConcurrentSessions, and does not hide failures.
